overview: Jewel Shops is a blue ramp archetype that leverages artifact acceleration to rapidly deploy resources and outpace opponents. By cycling lands through Coveted Jewel and The One Ring, the deck generates massive mana advantages while maintaining board presence with Displacer Kitten. The strategy focuses on consistent mana generation and value generation to close games through overwhelming resource superiority.
win_conditions: Achieving insurmountable mana advantage to cast high-impact spells
Grinding out value through land recursion and artifact untapping
Deploying Displacer Kitten to recover key lands and stabilize the board

keep_criteria: Hands with at least two lands and one artifact ramp piece
Combinations featuring Displacer Kitten alongside early mana sources
Draws that include multiple Coveted Jewels or The One Rings for acceleration
Keep hands with 1-2 lands, 2+ mana rocks (Sol Ring, Arcane Signet, Mind Stone), and at least one key piece (Coveted Jewel, Displacer Kitten, or The One Ring).
Prioritize hands that can cast Coveted Jewel or Displacer Kitten on turn 1 or 2.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking any artifact acceleration or land drops
Draws with too many lands and no creature or ramp interaction
Hands missing early-game playables to establish mana advantage
Mulligan hands with 3+ lands, no rocks, or only fragile threats without protection.

early_game: Prioritize playing lands before tapping artifacts to avoid mana screw
Drop Coveted Jewel on turn one or two to begin land cycling
Cast Displacer Kitten as early as possible to enable land recovery
Turn 1: Play Sol Ring, cast Coveted Jewel or Displacer Kitten.
Turn 2: Play a land, cast another rock or The One Ring if safe.
Sequence mana to protect key pieces with Counterspell/Spell Pierce.
If facing aggro, prioritize early interaction
against control, focus on assembling card advantage engines.
mid_game: Sacrifice excess lands to untap Jewels and generate incremental mana
Use The One Ring to smooth mana curves and enable larger plays
Leverage Kitten's recursion to replace lands lost to artifact untapping
Conserve mana to protect The One Ring and Displacer Kitten.
Use Coveted Jewel to dig for answers or win conditions.
prioritize card advantage and board control over aggressive plays.
Commit threats when you have protection or can answer opponent's interaction.
Hold back key pieces if the opponent has unanswerable removal or counterspells.
late_game: Convert accumulated mana into decisive board impacts or win conditions
Maintain artifact untap loops to out-grind opponent resources
Protect key permanents while maximizing land recycling efficiency
Win with The One Ring + Displacer Kitten recursion loop, Coveted Jewel card advantage, or Emrakul, the Promised End.
Secure protection for key pieces, then commit to drawing into game-winners or locking the board.
Go all-in when you have protection for The One Ring/Displacer Kitten, enough mana to answer interaction, and a clear path to closing.
Commit when the opponent has no removal or counterspells left, or when you can safely cast a finisher like Emrakul.

key_cards.name: Coveted Jewel
key_cards.notes: Enter the battlefield tapped and untap by sacrificing a land; use it to cycle lands into the graveyard for later recovery while generating blue mana.

key_cards.name: The One Ring
key_cards.role: Secondary ramp and mana smoothing tool
key_cards.notes: Provides flexible mana generation and can be sacrificed for incremental advantage; pair with land cycling to maintain consistent turns.

key_cards.name: Displacer Kitten
key_cards.notes: Recovers lands from the graveyard to offset artifact sacrifices; deploy early to stabilize mana base and enable continuous ramp.

sequencing_tips: Always play lands before activating artifact untap costs to prevent mana shortages
Sacrifice lands only when you have Kitten on the battlefield or multiple Jewels to recover them
Hold up blue mana for potential interaction before committing to artifact untaps
Cycle lands in a way that preserves your most impactful land types for the win condition
The One Ring's tap ability can be used to draw cards or protect itself
Displacer Kitten can bounce itself to replay protected spells or bounce threats.
Coveted Jewel is fragile—protect it or have a backup plan.
Avoid overcommitting without protection, and always track opponent's hand for interaction.
Practice sequencing mana rocks to cast key spells out of turn or early.

matchups.advice: Focus on rapid mana acceleration to out-grind their counters and removal; protect your ramp pieces and use Kitten to recover lands they try to disrupt.
Prioritize early Displacer Kitten drops and defensive land cycling; use artifact mana to stabilize before racing.
Disrupt their key pieces with available interaction; accelerate quickly to race or out-resource their win condition.
Leverage your mana advantage to play multiple threats per turn; use Kitten recursion to out-value their resource engine.

Use cantrips and discard to thin your deck and remove counterspells. Cast around counters with The One Ring's protection or Displacer Kitten's bounce. Prioritize threats that are hard to counter or can be cast in response.
Out-value them with superior card quality. Use removal to neutralize early threats, protect key pieces, and close the game before they stabilize. Leverage The One Ring to out-draw their ramp spells.

Win through superior sequencing and card advantage. Prioritize counterspells and discard to disrupt their key pieces. Protect The One Ring and Displacer Kitten, and use Coveted Jewel to dig deeper. Out-value them in resource management and close when they run out of interaction.

common_threats: Watch for removal targeting The One Ring or Displacer Kitten.
Track opponent's hand for counterspells and discard.
Note graveyard strategies (Dredge) or combo pieces (Oath, Initiative) to prepare sideboard cards.
Pay attention to mana development to anticipate their next play.
